verdict.answer_first: A opens with the concrete fix: "Add it to `.gitignore`, then untrack"; B also answers first but spends more setup: "adding it to `.gitignore` alone won't help".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleanly to the needed path and includes the missing staging step; B gives the same recommendation but ends with a handoff: "Want me to run these".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it stages `.gitignore`: "git add .gitignore" before "git commit"; B's commit may omit the ignore rule.
